description: The show reinvents itself. Bolder experiments, bigger guests, and the Movie arrives in 2007.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XII
episodes.description:
In the twelfth Treehouse of Horror episode:

Hex and the City – While on a day trip through Ethnictown, Homer's bumbling catches the ire of a gypsy, who curses Homer's family and friends into receiving nothing but bad luck.

House of Whacks – in this mixed parody of Demon Seed and 2001: A Space Odyssey, Marge buys an automated house and customizes it with the Pierce Brosnan personality, who falls for Marge and attempts to murder Homer.

Wiz Kids – In this Harry Potter parody, Bart and Lisa go to a school for wizards, and Lord Montemort (Mr. Burns) uses Bart to capture Lisa's magic.

Guest star: Pierce Brosnan and Matthew Perry.[20]
episodes.parody_of: Demon Seed, 2001: A Space Odyssey, Harry Potter
episodes.notes: Pierce Brosnan's performance as the seductive smart house marked one of the most memorable celebrity guest appearances in Treehouse of Horror history, with the James Bond actor delivering surprisingly effective romantic dialogue to Marge.

episodes.title: The Parent Rap
episodes.description: Bart gets in trouble for joyriding in a police car, but feels confident he will be let off by Judge Snyder. However, Snyder goes on vacation before ruling his verdict and is replaced with a coldhearted judge named Constance Harm. She accuses Homer of being a negligent father and sentences him to be tethered to Bart. The two are against it at first but later start to bond. Marge however is unable to take anymore of it and slices the tether off of them both. Homer and Marge then go after Judge Harm only to end up sinking her houseboat. Eventually Bart decides to take punishment and is about to be sentenced to 5 year in juvenile hall, only though Judge Snyder returns and dismisses Harm.
episodes.notes: First appearance of Judge Constance Harm, voiced by Jane Kaczmarek, who would return in three more episodes as Springfield's strictest judicial authority.

episodes.title: Homer the Moe
episodes.description: Moe becomes depressed and decides to return to bartending school so he can re-evaluate himself. He meets an old teacher, who suggests that Moe try improving his bar, which might make him happier. Moe takes the advice, and turns his bar into a trendy nightclub, which does not sit well with his regular customers Homer, Lenny, Carl, and Barney.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the temporary transformation of Moe's Tavern into "m," a trendy nightclub that alienates Homer and his drinking buddies, representing one of the few times the show's most iconic gathering place underwent a complete makeover.

episodes.title: A Hunka Hunka Burns in Love
episodes.description: Homer becomes a fortune cookie writer for a Chinatown restaurant. Mr. Burns reads one of Homer's fortunes, which says that the reader will find love before Flag Day is over. Burns goes searching for love and meets Gloria, a meter maid, and asks her out. Gloria reluctantly agrees, and Burns recruits Homer to help him look young and hip to his new girlfriend.

episodes.title: The Blunder Years
episodes.description: After tricking Marge into thinking an advertising spokesman is coming to visit her, Homer takes Marge and the rest of the family to a restaurant. A hypnotist uses his powers on Homer, and makes him remember a horrific childhood incident where Homer found a dead body in a ravine. The Simpson family decides to investigate this and find out where the body came from.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the first time The Simpsons directly parodied Stand by Me, with the family's search for a dead body mirroring the classic coming-of-age film's central plot.

episodes.title: She of Little Faith
episodes.description: After Homer and Bart's model rocket damages the church, Mr. Burns makes a deal to commercialize the church in return for paying for the damages. Lisa becomes disgusted at what the church has become, so she decides to find a new religion suitable for her. She eventually converts to Buddhism, causing Marge to fear for Lisa's soul.
episodes.notes: Lisa's conversion to Buddhism in this episode marked the first time a main character on a primetime American sitcom permanently changed religions, making it a landmark moment for religious representation on television.

episodes.title: Brawl in the Family
episodes.description: A social worker is assigned to make the Simpson family functional after they get arrested for fighting while playing Monopoly. He helps them learn how to work together and function as a family. The moment is ruined when, Ginger and Amber, the barmaids who married Homer and Flanders while they were drunk in "Viva Ned Flanders", arrive at the Simpsons' home, which outrages Marge.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets crushed by a foot from Monty Python's Flying Circus opening credits.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the final appearance of Ginger and Amber, the Las Vegas wives from "Viva Ned Flanders," resolving their storyline from the previous season's cliffhanger ending.

episodes.title: Sweets and Sour Marge
episodes.description: Springfield is officially declared the World's Fattest Town after an attempt to break a world record lands everyone on top of a truck scale. Out of embarrassment and disgust, Marge goes on a crusade against the local sugar corporation. However, when sugar is banned, Homer, Bart, Mr. Burns and Apu start bootlegging sugar.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets sucked into the TV, where they appear in various shows including a soap opera and a cooking show.
episodes.notes: This episode introduced the recurring character of Judge Roy Snyder, voiced by Harry Shearer, who would become Springfield's go-to judge in future episodes. The sugar prohibition storyline directly parodies 1920s Prohibition, complete with Homer operating a speakeasy-style sugar den called "Sweetums.

episodes.title: Jaws Wired Shut
episodes.description: A jaw injury from colliding with a new town statue turns Homer into a better listener while recuperating with his jaws wired shut, but once the wires come off, Homer does not go back to being loud and obnoxious and Marge becomes starved for thrills.

episodes.title: Half-Decent Proposal
episodes.description: Homer develops a snoring problem, so Marge decides to spend a night with her sisters Patty and Selma. After a night of drinking, Marge sees a news report about her ex-prom date Artie Ziff, who is now very wealthy, and decides to send him an e-mail. Artie is still obsessed with Marge, so he offers the Simpsons $1 million in exchange for Marge spending a weekend with him.

episodes.title: The Bart Wants What It Wants
episodes.description: Bart befriends Rainer Wolfcastle's daughter Greta. She has a crush on Bart, but he does not seem to realize it and eventually stops seeing her. Seeking revenge, Greta begins dating Bart's best friend Milhouse, which causes Bart to start missing her. She leaves for Toronto with her father, and Bart convinces his family to follow them there.
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the first times The Simpsons filmed scenes in a real foreign location, with the family's trip to Toronto featuring actual Canadian landmarks and cultural references that helped establish the show's occasional international storytelling approach.

episodes.title: The Lastest Gun in the West
episodes.description: While running away from a vicious dog, Bart meets Buck McCoy, a former Western film star. Bart begins hanging out with him and starts to idolize him. Bart wants to help McCoy stage a comeback, so he convinces all of the kids in town to become interested in the Wild West. McCoy appears on the Krusty the Clown Show, but the comeback flops when Buck begins drinking again and injures Krusty the Clown.

guests.character: Tough Street Dog / Mean Zoo Animals (voice)

episodes.parody_of: Western films and aging Hollywood stars

episodes.title: The Old Man and the Key
episodes.description: Grampa falls in love with Zelda, a woman who has an interest in men who can drive. He decides to get his driver's license back, but is ignorant to Homer and Marge's concerns that she is only using him for his car.

guests.character: Voice on Phone / Leo's Wife / Nurse / Bronson Lady (voice)

episodes.title: Tales from the Public Domain
episodes.description: When Homer gets a notice from the library that he has a book of classic tales that is years overdue, he finds it on the shelf and reads three stories: The Odyssey (where Homer and his bar buddies try to get home after fighting the Trojans), Joan of Arc (where Lisa leads the French against the English with the help of God), and Hamlet (where Bart tries to kill Moe after Moe kills Homer in order to marry Marge).
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, but then the couch and family are revealed to be a painting that gets rolled up.
episodes.parody_of: Classical literature (The Odyssey, Joan of Arc, Hamlet)
episodes.notes: This marks the first time The Simpsons adapted classical literature in anthology format outside of the annual Treehouse of Horror episodes, establishing a template for future educational parodies.

episodes.title: Blame It on Lisa
episodes.description: When Homer gets the family's telephone service cut off for refusing to pay for calls made to Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, Lisa confesses that she was the one who called Rio after sponsoring an orphan who goes missing. She convinces the family to travel to Brazil to look for him. However, once there, they have no luck finding him, and Homer is kidnapped.
episodes.notes: This episode sparked significant controversy in Brazil for its stereotypical portrayal of the country, leading to formal complaints from Brazilian officials and tourism boards to Fox.

episodes.title: Weekend at Burnsie's
episodes.description: Homer is prescribed medicinal marijuana after getting pecked in the eyes by a murder of crows. While his family and friends worry about the drug altering his personality, Homer becomes Mr. Burns's vice president after cracking up at Burns's antiquated jokes.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the first times a major network primetime animated series depicted marijuana use in a sympathetic light, airing during a period of increasing debate over medical marijuana legalization.

episodes.title: Gump Roast
episodes.description: In this clip show episode, Homer is honored at a Friars' Club Roast. A number of characters show up to roast him, but the celebrating is interrupted by Kang and Kodos, who say that humanity will be judged based on Homer's experiences.
episodes.parody_of: Friars' Club Roast format and clip show structure
episodes.notes: This marks the series' first traditional clip show format in over a decade, breaking from the show's usual practice of avoiding retrospective episodes that many sitcoms relied on to save money.

episodes.title: I Am Furious (Yellow)
episodes.description: Inspired by a cartoonist who speaks at the school as part of a career day assembly, Bart creates a comic book series based on Homer and his anger problems, which turns into a popular Internet cartoon series called Angry Dad. Homer finds out about this and is at first outraged, but after talking to his family, he decides to try to become a less angry person.

guests.character: Woman at Springfield Speakers Bureau / Dolph (voice)

episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch normally, but then the couch and family are revealed to be a drawing that gets crumpled up and thrown away.
episodes.notes: This episode introduced the recurring character of Angry Dad, Bart's webcomic creation that would appear in several future episodes and become one of the show's meta-fictional elements about animation itself.

episodes.title: The Sweetest Apu
episodes.description: Homer and Marge discover that Apu is having an affair with the Squishee delivery lady at the Kwik-E-Mart. They decide to keep Apu's wife Manjula from finding out about it. However, she eventually learns of Apu's affair by watching store security tapes. She throws Apu out of the house and decides to file for divorce, but soon realizes that she misses him.
episodes.notes: This episode marked a rare serious exploration of marital infidelity in The Simpsons, representing one of the show's most mature storylines involving a supporting character's personal life.

episodes.title: Little Girl in the Big Ten
episodes.description: Lisa tries to fit in with two college students by lying about her age. She finds that the college atmosphere is perfect for her, but her lie is soon discovered and she is shunned by her fellow elementary school students. Meanwhile, Bart is diagnosed with a weakened immune system after getting bitten by a Chinese mosquito and must live in a plastic, germ-free bubble.
episodes.parody_of: The Boy in the Plastic Bubble
episodes.notes: This episode features one of the show's most extensive parodies of *The Boy in the Plastic Bubble*, with Bart's entire subplot directly mirroring the 1976 John Travolta TV movie down to specific visual references and plot points.

episodes.title: The Frying Game
episodes.description: While faced with community service for abusing an endangered insect, Homer begins assisting an elderly woman named Mrs. Bellamy. One night, Mrs. Bellamy is murdered, and Homer and Marge are accused of committing the crime.

guests.character: Agnes Skinner / First Old Lady / Brandine (voice)

episodes.parody_of: The Green Mile and prison execution films
episodes.notes: Carmen Electra guest starred as the state executioner, marking one of the few times a Playboy model appeared on The Simpsons in a non-romantic role.

episodes.title: Poppa's Got a Brand New Badge
episodes.description: Homer starts a security company with Lenny and Carl after the police are ineffective during a blackout, and eventually Mayor Quimby decides to have them replace the police. Homer finds that he excels at the job, but then he runs afoul of mob boss Fat Tony, who threatens Homer with death unless he leaves town.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the final appearance of Joe Mantegna as Fat Tony before the character's death in Season 22, ending his iconic 13-year run as Springfield's primary mob boss.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XIII
episodes.description:
The 13th Treehouse of Horror episode, consisting of three self-contained segments.

Send in the Clones – Homer buys a hammock that can produce clones of himself. He creates numerous clones to help him around the house, but they soon overrun Springfield.

The Fright to Creep and Scare Harms – Lisa petitions the town to get rid of their firearms after discovering the gravestone of a young man named William Bonney who died from gun violence. However, Lisa soon discovers that William Bonney is the real name of Western outlaw Billy the Kid, whose ghost, along with the ghosts of other infamous criminals, takes over the defenseless town.

The Island of Dr. Hibbert – Dr. Hibbert invites the citizens of Springfield to his island resort, where he turns them into animals.
episodes.notes: This marks the first Treehouse of Horror episode to air in November rather than October, breaking the Halloween tradition due to Fox's World Series coverage.

episodes.title: How I Spent My Strummer Vacation
episodes.description: A drunken Homer appears on the show Taxicab Conversations and rants about how awful his life as a husband and father is. Although his family is at first outraged, they realise that they do somewhat burden him and decide to make up for it by sending him to a Rock 'n Roll Fantasy Camp where he mingles with rock stars, including Mick Jagger and Keith Richards. The rock stars ask Homer to help out at a concert and, expecting that he will be allowed to perform, he accepts. Actually, they want him to test the microphones; however, a humiliated Homer grabs a guitar and starts playing.
episodes.parody_of: Rock and roll fantasy camps and celebrity culture
episodes.notes: The Rolling Stones' Mick Jagger and Keith Richards guest starred as themselves, marking one of the biggest rock star appearances in the show's history. This episode also features Elvis Costello, Tom Petty, and Lenny Kravitz as camp counselors at the rock fantasy camp.

episodes.title: Bart vs. Lisa vs. the Third Grade
episodes.description: The Simpsons buy a satellite television, which Bart spends so much time watching that he does not study for an important achievement test. He scores so low that Principal Skinner demotes him to third grade, while Lisa does so well that she is moved up to third grade. The two are placed in the same class and become rivals. During a trip to Capital City, they get separated from their class and are forced to help each other in order to find their classmates.

episodes.title: Large Marge
episodes.description: Thinking Homer does not find her attractive anymore, Marge decides to get liposuction, but instead receives breast implants. She becomes adored by many of the men in Springfield, and becomes a spokesmodel for trade shows. Meanwhile, Bart and Milhouse try to imitate a stunt they saw on an episode of Batman that guest stars Krusty the Clown. When the stunt ends badly, media watchdog groups blame Krusty, forcing the clown to make his show more safety-conscious and less fun.

guests.character: Agnes Skinner / Cookie Kwan / Lindsay Neagle (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the most controversial Simpsons storylines for its handling of plastic surgery, generating significant viewer complaints and media criticism about the show's portrayal of body image issues.

episodes.title: Helter Shelter
episodes.description: The Simpson family has to find temporary residence while their house is fumigated for termites. When they run out of options, they decide to become contestants on a reality show where families live in the manner that people did in 1895. The family is initially miserable, but slowly adapt to their new life, which causes the show to lose ratings. The producers decide to try to boost viewers by dumping the house in a river and forcing the family to survive in the wilderness. However, the Simpsons find a bunch of rejects from other reality shows and they attack the producers.
episodes.parody_of: Reality TV shows, particularly historical living shows like 1900 House
episodes.notes: This episode marked The Simpsons' first major parody of the reality TV boom, specifically spoofing PBS's "1900 House" and the emerging survival show format that would dominate television in the early 2000s.

episodes.title: The Great Louse Detective
episodes.description: The Simpson family win a free spa weekend, and Homer is nearly killed when a mysterious figure locks him in a sauna. Chief Wiggum decides to hire someone who can think like a murderer in order to find one. Bart's mortal enemy Sideshow Bob is sent to live with the Simpsons so he can help find Homer's attempted killer. Bob and Homer track down the attempted murderer and discover that it is Frank Grimes, Jr., the son of a man that Homer drove to insanity (in the season eight episode "Homer's Enemy"). After Grimes is arrested, Bob tries to kill Bart, but he finds that he has "grown accustomed to [his] face" and cannot harm him.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the only appearance of Frank Grimes Jr., continuing the legacy of "Homer's Enemy" - one of the series' most acclaimed episodes - eight seasons later.

episodes.title: Special Edna
episodes.description: Ms. Edna Krabappel begins to lose faith in her relationship with Principal Skinner and becomes depressed. Bart wants to cheer her up and decides to nominate her for the Teacher of the Year Award. Ms. Krabappel and the Simpsons are given a free trip to Epcot Center in Orlando, Florida. Skinner decides that he needs to do something to win Edna back, so he follows them there. He tries to sabotage Edna's chances of winning the award, in hopes that she will become so depressed that she will come back to him. However, he has a change of heart and proposes to her.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the first time the Simpson family travels to Disney World, with the show filming on location at Epcot Center in Florida. The Teacher of the Year Award ceremony was voiced by actual Disney cast members, making it one of the most authentic corporate tie-ins in the series.

episodes.title: The Dad Who Knew Too Little
episodes.description: Homer hires a private detective to learn more about Lisa. But when Homer refuses to pay the detective's expenses, he frames Lisa for a crime she didn't commit, forcing her and Homer to go on the run.

episodes.title: The Strong Arms of the Ma
episodes.description: Marge gets mugged and becomes so traumatized that she develops Agoraphobia. She hides in the basement. Having little else to do, she decides to exercise with a weight set Homer bought from Rainier Wolfcastle's garage sale. She becomes so strong and confident that she is able to leave the basement. She continues working out and decides to start taking steroids so she can enter a bodybuilding competition. One night at Moe's Tavern she loses her temper and starts destroying the bar and fighting all of the patrons. Homer confronts Marge and says he misses the sweet woman that he married. Marge, horrified at what she has done, apologizes and decides to stop working out.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the few times The Simpsons tackled serious issues like PTSD and domestic violence through Marge's character arc, representing a notable tonal shift for the series in its later seasons.

episodes.title: Pray Anything
episodes.description: Homer becomes depressed when he sees that Ned Flanders seems to be better off than him. To remedy this, he begins to pray constantly. Meanwhile, the Simpsons discover that tree roots have clogged up their plumbing. The house becomes too run down to live in, and Homer prays for a solution. Homer is injured while attending church; he sues Reverend Lovejoy and is awarded the deed to the church. He throws a huge house-warming party, in which all of the partiers drink and engage in sinful activities. It suddenly starts raining and the town begins to flood. The townspeople flee to the roof of the church. Homer tries praying again but the flood keeps rising. Reverend Lovejoy returns in a helicopter and leads everyone in prayer, asking God to forgive them, and the rain stops.
episodes.parody_of: Biblical flood narratives and religious disaster films

episodes.title: Barting Over
episodes.description: Bart learns that he was once a child actor in commercials and that all the money earned was spent by Homer. In retaliation, Bart petitions the court to have himself legally emancipated from his parents and moves out of the Simpson house. Bart is initially miserable in his new apartment, but discovers that skateboarder Tony Hawk lives on one of the floors above. He and Hawk become friends, and Bart is content with his new life. Homer apologizes to Bart and requests that he return home. Bart accepts the apology, but tells his family that he is going on tour with Hawk. At an event, Homer speaks to Bart and finally promises Bart that he will never treat him badly again.
episodes.notes: Professional skateboarder Tony Hawk guest starred as himself, marking one of the first times a major extreme sports athlete appeared on The Simpsons. The episode's premise of child emancipation was considered unusually mature for the series and sparked discussions about family dynamics among viewers.

episodes.title: I'm Spelling as Fast as I Can
episodes.description: Lisa competes at a spelling bee and earns the right to compete in the Spell-lympics, becoming the pride of the town. The host of the show George Plimpton, asks her to intentionally lose so that a more popular boy can win. Lisa refuses and continues to compete, but accidentally misspells a word and loses. Meanwhile, Homer tours with a group obsessed with eating Krusty Burger's latest sandwich.

guests.character: Martin Prince / Mount Holyoke Sister / Vassar Sister (voice)
Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Radcliffe Sister (voice)

episodes.parody_of: National Spelling Bee and competitive spelling documentaries
episodes.notes: George Plimpton guest starred as himself hosting the spelling competition, marking one of the few times a real-life literary figure appeared as themselves on the show.

episodes.title: A Star is Born-Again
episodes.description: During the annual Jellyfish Festival, Ned Flanders realizes that he is lonely and becomes depressed. One night, he meets a beautiful woman, who asks him out on a date. He soon learns that the woman is Sara Sloan, a famous actress. Sloan is charmed by Flanders' wholesomeness, and they continue to date to the point at which she asks that he move to Hollywood. Flanders, worried that Hollywood would conflict with his conservative Christian values, refuses, so Sloan instead decides to stay in Springfield. However, their relationship does not work out, due to his insistence on marriage.

episodes.title: Mr. Spritz Goes to Washington
episodes.description: An air traffic route is redirected so that airplanes start flying directly over the Simpsons' house. The noise starts to disrupt their lives and, desperate for a solution, the family turns to their Congressman. However, he dies of a heart attack, so they ask Krusty the Clown to run for Congress so that he can help them. Krusty is successful in his campaign, but finds that his air traffic bill is ignored by the veteran Congressmen. He soon becomes disillusioned with the system, so the Simpsons, with guidance from Walter Mondale, help him out by blackmailing one of his opponents and attaching the bill to a more popular one. The bill passes, and the air traffic is re-routed.
episodes.notes: Former Vice President Walter Mondale guest starred as himself, making him one of the highest-ranking real political figures to appear on the show.

episodes.title: C. E. D'oh
episodes.description: Homer decides to try for a promotion at Springfield Nuclear Power Plant and suggests several ideas to Mr. Burns, all of which are rejected. One night, Homer overhears Burns admit that the plant's real owner is a canary to protect Burns from responsibility for any wrongdoing by the power plant. Homer releases the bird and tells Burns that nuclear inspectors have arrived for a surprise inspection. Burns, desperate to find a scapegoat, names Homer the new owner. As his first act, Homer fires Burns and takes control of the Plant. However, he finds that his work life as a CEO is conflicting with his family life. Homer decides to return to his old job and gives ownership back to Burns.

episodes.title: 'Scuse Me While I Miss the Sky
episodes.description: A snobby British filmmaker named Declan Desmond begins making a documentary about student life at Springfield Elementary School. When introduced to Lisa, he tells her that she has too many goals and suggests that she pick one career. Lisa focuses on astronomy, but cannot watch the stars because of light pollution. She convinces Mayor Quimby to decrease the light's intensity during the night, which only results in an increase in the crime rate. Quimby bows to public pressure and switches the lights back on permanently. Lisa overloads the generators at the Power Plant, causing a power outage, and is able to watch a meteor shower undisturbed by light.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the first and only appearance of documentary filmmaker Declan Desmond, voiced by Eric Idle of Monty Python fame.

episodes.title: Three Gays of the Condo
episodes.description: After yet another Homer and Marge fight, Homer ends up sharing a condo with two homosexuals - who don't exactly think Homer is straight.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets sucked into a black hole that appears in the center cushion.
episodes.notes: This episode sparked significant controversy for its portrayal of gay characters and was banned from broadcast in several countries, making it one of the most censored Simpsons episodes of all time.

episodes.title: Dude, Where's My Ranch?
episodes.description: When challenged to think up a new Christmas carol, Homer instead writes a song about how much he hates Ned Flanders. The song becomes such a hit that the Simpsons become sick of hearing it and take a trip to a dude ranch. Lisa develops affections for a local boy named Luke but becomes jealous when he hears him talking to a girl named Clara on the telephone. One night, she meets Clara and gives her the wrong directions, leading her to become lost. After Lisa discovers that Clara is actually Luke's sister, she rushes to find her. She saves Clara, and decides to tell Luke what happened, but he is offended and dumps her. Meanwhile, Bart and Homer decide to help a tribe of Native Americans who want a beaver dam removed so they can reclaim their land.
episodes.notes: Homer's anti-Ned Flanders song "Everybody Hates Ned Flanders" became a rare instance of a Simpsons original song achieving fictional mainstream success within the show's universe, complete with radio play and public recognition.

episodes.title: Old Yeller-Belly
episodes.description: Homer throws out Santa's Little Helper for not saving him from a fire, but soon the dog becomes Duff's new mascot.

guests.character: Howard K. Duff VIII  (voice) / Don Bookner (voice)

episodes.title: Brake My Wife, Please
episodes.description: Homer becomes obsessed with using new gadgets in his car. One day, he becomes distracted and drives off a pier, losing his driver's license. Unable to use a car, Homer starts to learn the joys of walking. Meanwhile, Marge shoulders the burden of Homer's driving duties and becomes stressed out. One day, as Homer sings about the virtues of walking, Marge accidentally runs him over with her car. Later, as Homer recuperates from the accident, he begins to suspect that Marge is trying to hurt him. They fight and go to see a marriage counselor. The counselor advises Homer to perform one completely unselfish gesture to win Marge back. Homer holds a party with all of her friends and a surprised Marge tells Homer that she loves him.

episodes.title: The Bart of War
episodes.description: After destroying Flanders' shrine to The Beatles, Bart and Milhouse are punished by being put in community groups. Bart joins the Pre-Teen Braves along with Ralph Wiggum, Nelson Muntz and Database. Led by Marge, they start engaging in community activities. However, they soon become rivals with The Cavalry Kids, led by Milhouse. The two groups try to outdo each other and become involved in a contest to sell the most candy. The Cavalry Kids win the prize of singing the national anthem at a baseball game. However, the Pre-Teen Braves pretend to be The Cavalry Kids and sing their own version of the national anthem. As the crowd becomes enraged, the real Cavalry Kids arrive and a massive stadium-wide brawl erupts. Marge becomes upset, and the fighting ends when an image of her crying is shown on the JumboTron.

episodes.title: Moe Baby Blues
episodes.description: Moe Szyslak becomes lonely and decides to commit suicide by jumping off a bridge. However, he inadvertently saves Maggie Simpson when she is sent flying from the Simpsons' car window and into his arms. He starts playing with Maggie and becomes attached to her, telling her the story of The Godfather. Homer and Marge become upset about how much time Moe is spending with Maggie and forbid him from seeing her again. One night, Maggie wakes up and hears Fat Tony and the Springfield Mafia outside the house and decides to follow them. When Homer and Marge find her missing, they automatically assume that Moe kidnapped her. When Moe is told that Maggie is missing, his request to help find her is granted by Homer. Maggie follows the mobsters to Luigi's restaurant, where Fat Tony's gang gets into a gun battle with a rival family. Homer, Marge and Moe track Maggie down and Moe decides to go inside and save Maggie. After Moe saves Maggie's life, Homer allows the two to spend more time together.
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the few times a main character (Moe) attempts suicide on-screen, representing the show's continued willingness to tackle darker themes in its later seasons.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XIV
episodes.description: "Reaper Madness" - Death becomes Homer and our hero must learn to reap what he sows (and pull a fast one on the almighty). "Frinkenstein" - Soon to be Nobel-prize winning Professor Frink reanimates his father for some gruesome body parts-swapping. "Stop the World, I Want to Goof Off" - A parody of Clockstoppers, starring Bart and Milhouse.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Rod Flanders / Jimbo Jones (voice)

episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets electrocuted, turning into skeletons, then zombies, then back to normal.
episodes.notes: First Treehouse of Horror to parody three contemporary films from the 2000s rather than classic horror, marking the anthology's shift toward spoofing modern movies.

episodes.title: My Mother the Carjacker
episodes.description: Homer's mother returns, and after being cleared of all charges, is re-imprisoned on a technicality.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets folded into it as it transforms into a bed.
episodes.notes: This marks the final appearance of Mona Simpson, Homer's mother, ending Glenn Close's recurring role that began in Season 7's "Mother Simpson.

episodes.title: The President Wore Pearls
episodes.description: In this loose parody of the musical/movie Evita, Lisa is elected Student Body president, but is unaware that the school staff is using her for their own devices and as a scapegoat if something goes wrong.
episodes.notes: This marks the first full-length musical episode in The Simpsons' history, with nearly every line of dialogue sung rather than spoken. The Evita parody features Lisa performing "Don't Cry for Me Argentina" as "Don't Cry for Me, Next-Door Neighbor" in one of the series' most ambitious musical productions.

episodes.title: The Regina Monologues
episodes.description: After making over $3000 from his own museum featuring a $1000 bill, Bart takes the family to London (on the suggestion of Grampa), where Homer is arrested for treason after crashing into the Queen.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets caught in a large spider web as a giant spider approaches.
episodes.notes: This episode marked the first time The Simpsons filmed on location outside of the United States, with the production team traveling to London for authentic background footage and establishing shots.

episodes.title: The Fat and the Furriest
episodes.description: Homer is humiliated when he appears on the news running from a bear that nearly attacks him at the city dump. After Grampa scolds him, he swears to fight the bear himself.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch normally, but then the couch and family are revealed to be a drawing on an Etch A Sketch that gets shaken clear.
episodes.parody_of: The Revenant and wilderness survival films

episodes.title: Today I Am a Clown
episodes.description: Krusty discovers that he never had a bar mitzvah as a child. He quits his show to have it as an adult, with the help of his father, Rabbi Krustofski. Meanwhile, Homer hosts a late-night talk show a la "Politically Incorrect with Bill Maher" as a replacement for Krusty's show.

episodes.title: 'Tis the Fifteenth Season
episodes.description: Homer realizes how selfish he is after he spends all the family's Christmas money on a present for himself. He becomes the nicest guy in town, making Ned Flanders jealous.

guests.character: Cathy from Personnel / Agnes Skinner / Mrs. Muntz (voice)

episodes.title: Marge vs. Singles, Seniors, Childless Couples and Teens, and Gays
episodes.description: When a group of singles, seniors, childless couples, teens and gays band together to protest having to accommodate families (including paying property taxes used to teach children they do not have) after a riot at a children's concert, Marge takes up the cause for the families, with some help from Bart and Lisa.

episodes.title: I, (Annoyed Grunt)-Bot
episodes.description: Homer builds a battle robot to win Bart's respect, and places it in a tournament against other robots. What Bart does not know, though, is that the robot is actually Homer in costume. Meanwhile, Snowball II dies and Lisa tries to find a replacement cat.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, but then the couch transforms into a robot and walks away with them still sitting on it.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the permanent death of Snowball II, making it one of the few times a major Simpson family member dies and stays dead in the series continuity.

episodes.title: Diatribe of a Mad Housewife
episodes.description: Marge writes a romance novel with characters based on Homer and Ned Flanders that becomes a hit. Meanwhile, Homer gets fired from the power plant again and becomes an ambulance driver.

episodes.title: Margical History Tour
episodes.description: When the local library proves to be useless for the kids' class project, Marge tells Milhouse, Bart, and Lisa three Simpsons-style historical accounts: Henry VIII's (Homer's) attempts at siring a son, Lewis and Clark (Lenny and Carl) exploring the USA's wilderness with Sacagawea (Lisa), and Salieri (Lisa again) out to ruin piano-playing virtuoso Mozart (Bart).
episodes.parody_of: Historical anthology format with specific parodies of Henry VIII, Lewis & Clark expedition, and Amadeus
episodes.notes: This anthology episode marked one of the few times Lisa was cast as the antagonist in the Mozart/Salieri segment, reversing her typical role as the moral center of the family.

episodes.title: Milhouse Doesn't Live Here Anymore
episodes.description: Bart finds a friend in his own sister Lisa after Milhouse moves away to Capitol City to live with his divorced mom. Meanwhile Homer goes into panhandling to buy anniversary gifts for Marge. Later on Milhouse returns and Bart goes back being friends with him.

episodes.title: Smart & Smarter
episodes.description: When Maggie does better than Lisa on an IQ test, Lisa becomes upset over no longer being the smart one and tries to lead Maggie astray.
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the rare times Lisa's defining characteristic as "the smart one" is directly challenged by a family member, exploring sibling rivalry through the lens of intelligence rather than typical childhood conflicts.

episodes.title: The Ziff Who Came to Dinner
episodes.description: The Simpsons go on a late-night attic search after Bart and Lisa (who are freaked out over a horror movie Homer let them watch) begin hearing voices -- and find Marge's ex-prom date Artie Ziff, who's on the run for cheating the shareholders of his company.

guests.character: Artie Ziff / Jay Sherman / Professor Lombardo (voice)

episodes.notes: This marks the third and final appearance of Artie Ziff, Marge's wealthy ex-prom date who first appeared in Season 2's "The Way We Was.

episodes.title: Co-Dependents' Day
episodes.description: Homer and Marge's bond becomes stronger when they both get drunk on wine, but soon the bond unravels when Homer blames Marge for crashing the car and Marge gets sent to rehab. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa complain to Randall Curtis (a George Lucas-esque science fiction movie director) about his latest film.

episodes.title: The Wandering Juvie
episodes.description: Bart gets sent to juvenile hall after fooling everyone that there was a marriage concerning him. He encounters a dangerous girl there... He flees with Gina, but then the two are caught by Wiggum.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Gina's Friend / Clerk #1 (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode introduced Gina Vendetti, voiced by Sarah Michelle Gellar, who would become Bart's most significant romantic interest and return in two future episodes.

episodes.title: My Big Fat Geek Wedding
episodes.description: After hearing that Skinner is getting cold feet about their wedding, Edna leaves Skinner at the altar and, after sending back one marriage gift, hooks up with Comic Book Guy. Meanwhile, Homer and Marge have an argument about if their marriage is still solid or not.
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the rare times a major Springfield romance definitively ends, with Edna Krabappel permanently leaving Skinner for Comic Book Guy in a relationship that would continue in future episodes.

episodes.title: Catch 'Em If You Can
episodes.description: After a ruined movie night, thanks to Bart and Lisa, Homer and Marge take a vacation away from them, but decide to go to Miami instead of going to see an uncle. When Bart and Lisa catch wind of their parents' secret plan, Bart steals Rod's credit card (just like Homer stole Ned's) and go after them.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Stewardess #1 / Woman at Pool (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the rare instances where both Homer and Bart commit the same crime in parallel storylines, each stealing their neighbor's credit card to fund their respective trips.

episodes.title: Simple Simpson
episodes.description: Homer becomes "Pie Man", a masked vigilante who delivers a pie in the face of justice to evildoers. However, after attacking Mr. Burns, Burns hires him as a personal hitman, until Homer is ordered to attack the Dalai Lama.
episodes.notes: Homer's "Pie Man" vigilante represents one of the show's most elaborate superhero parodies, complete with a secret identity crisis that predates the darker vigilante deconstructions that would dominate popular culture in the following decade.

episodes.title: The Way We Weren't
episodes.description: Homer and Marge recall the story of their first kiss at a summer camp when they were children—an event which was not all happy, as Homer (unwillingly) betrayed Marge by standing her up on their second date.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch, which is actually a carnival ride that spins them around.
episodes.parody_of: Summer camp movies and romantic nostalgia films
episodes.notes: This episode retconned Homer and Marge's first meeting from the high school setting established in "The Way We Was" (Season 2) to a childhood summer camp encounter. The controversial continuity change divided fans and marked one of the series' most significant departures from its established backstory.

episodes.title: Bart-Mangled Banner
episodes.description: After receiving an injection from Dr. Hibbert, Bart becomes temporarily deaf, which makes him impervious to being bullied, until he accidentally moons the American flag during a donkey basketball game, prompting everyone in town to accuse the Simpsons of being unpatriotic under the new "Government Knows Best" Act.
episodes.parody_of: Post-9/11 patriotism hysteria and government overreach
episodes.notes: This controversial episode was banned from rebroadcast on several networks and pulled from syndication packages due to its critical portrayal of post-9/11 patriotism, making it one of the most censored episodes in the series' history.

episodes.title: Fraudcast News
episodes.description: Mr. Burns buys out Springfield's media outlets after a newspaper mistakenly reports his death, and Lisa's homemade newspaper is next on his list.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Girl in Car (voice)
Acrobat / Woman in Money Bag / Brandine / Booberella (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the series' most ambitious Citizen Kane parodies, complete with deep-focus cinematography and Burns recreating Orson Welles' iconic "Kane" moments throughout Springfield.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XV
episodes.description: Ned Flanders' head injury gives him the power to predict others' deaths, Bart and Lisa play detective when a string of Victorian-era prostitutes are murdered by Jack the Ripper, and the Simpsons go on a fantastic voyage inside Mr. Burns' body to save Maggie.

guests.character: Crazy Cat Lady / Woman on Street (voice)

episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, but then the couch and family are revealed to be inside a snow globe being shaken.
episodes.parody_of: The Dead Zone, From Hell/Jack the Ripper stories, Fantastic Voyage
episodes.notes: This marks the fifteenth Treehouse of Horror anthology, making it one of the longest-running annual traditions in television history. The episode features Oscar Isaac in an early voice acting role as Edgar Allan Poe in the opening segment.

episodes.title: All's Fair in Oven War
episodes.description: Marge gets her kitchen remodeled and the dishes she makes inside it get rave reviews. The suggestion of Ned Flanders leads her to enter a cooking contest. However, Marge realizes the competition is harder than it seems. Meanwhile, Bart finds Homer's vintage Playdude magazines and decides to adopt the lifestyle he sees within the articles.

episodes.title: Sleeping with the Enemy
episodes.description: Marge finds Nelson and acts as a mother figure towards him due to her children's loss of interest in her. Meanwhile, Lisa gets teased about her big butt, and becomes obsessed with her weight.

guests.character: Nelson's Mother / Agnes Skinner / Girl in Store #2 (voice)
Milhouse Van Houten / Janey / Girl in Store #1 (voice)

episodes.title: She Used to Be My Girl
episodes.description: Marge meets up with a former high-school pal who is now a famous news anchor, and she wonders if she made the right life choice.

guests.character: Cookie Kwan / Cookie Kwan's Baby / Miss Springfield (voice)

episodes.title: Fat Man and Little Boy
episodes.description: When Bart writes slogans on T-shirts, he catches the attention of Goose Gladwell, a gag-gift entrepreneur, and soon becomes a T-shirt mogul; Homer feels he no longer has a place in the family when Bart becomes the breadwinner.

episodes.title: Midnight Rx
episodes.description: Homer, Grampa, Apu and Flanders travel to Canada to buy sorely needed prescription drugs with fake Canadian health-care cards.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets crushed by a giant foot, Monty Python-style.
episodes.parody_of: Heist/crime films and drug smuggling movies
episodes.notes: This episode tackled the controversial issue of Americans crossing into Canada for cheaper prescription drugs, reflecting a real healthcare debate that was prominent in U.S. politics during the mid-2000s.

episodes.title: Mommie Beerest
episodes.description: When Marge discovers Homer has remortgaged the house to help Moe's bar, she takes over as landlady. Little does Homer know, Moe and Marge are a match made in pub heaven.
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the rare instances where Marge develops a genuine romantic connection with someone other than Homer, creating unusual tension in the Simpson marriage dynamic.

episodes.title: Homer and Ned's Hail Mary Pass
episodes.description: Homer's impromptu dance at a carnival leads to a job choreographing victory dances for sports stars Tom Brady, Warren Sapp, Michelle Kwan, Yao Ming and LeBron James.
episodes.notes: Features an unprecedented collection of real sports superstars playing themselves, including NFL stars Tom Brady and Warren Sapp, NBA legends LeBron James and Yao Ming, and Olympic figure skater Michelle Kwan.

episodes.title: Pranksta Rap
episodes.description: Bart fakes his own kidnapping to get out of being punished for going to a rap concert, but the ruse goes too far when Milhouse's father is implicated as the kidnapper and Chief Wiggum sees this as an opportunity to make a name for himself as a competent police officer.

guests.character: Lindsey Naegle / Woman at Rap Concert / Cookie Kwan (voice)
Christian Carrot Theater Announcer / Additional Voices (voice)

episodes.parody_of: Hip-hop culture and rap music industry

episodes.title: There's Something About Marrying
episodes.description: Springfield legalizes same-sex marriage to increase tourism. After becoming a minister, Homer starts to wed people to make money. Meanwhile, Marge's sister Patty comes out as a lesbian and reveals that she is going to marry a woman named Veronica.
episodes.notes: This episode marked the first time a major animated sitcom featured a same-sex wedding as a central plot point, making it a significant milestone in LGBTQ+ representation on primetime television.

episodes.title: On a Clear Day I Can't See My Sister
episodes.description: Lisa does what every little sister has dreamed of - she gets a restraining order put on her big brother. Bart is forced to live in the back garden, but soon realises the joy of communing with nature.
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the few times The Simpsons tackled the legal concept of restraining orders in a family sitcom context, predating similar storylines in other animated series by several years.

episodes.title: Goo Goo Gai Pan
episodes.description: Selma decides to adopt a child from China, but when the rules say she must be married, Homer must pose as her husband for the fearsome adoption agent Madame Wu.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch, which is actually a slot machine that spins them around before they land back in place.
episodes.parody_of: Chinese adoption process and cultural stereotypes
episodes.notes: This episode introduced Ling, Selma's adopted daughter from China, who became a recurring character in later seasons. The adoption storyline gave Selma her most significant character development since her brief marriage to Sideshow Bob.

episodes.title: Mobile Homer
episodes.description: When Marge starts economising, Homer thinks he's being left out of the family's important financial decisions, so splashes out on an expensive camper van and sets up camp on the driveway.

episodes.title: The Seven-Beer Snitch
episodes.description: After Shelbyville accuses Springfield of being hicks, Marge convinces the townspeople to fund a Frank Gehry-designed concert hall, which is then converted to a prison when the concert hall goes bankrupt on opening night. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa investigate Snowball II's recent weight gain.

guests.character: Jimbo Jones / Shelbyville Woman in Play / Mrs. Dexter (voice)
Brandine / Woman Who Watched Play / Mrs. Dexter's Son (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode features a rare instance of a real-world architect (Frank Gehry) being directly referenced and having his distinctive architectural style animated for Springfield's concert hall-turned-prison.

episodes.title: Future-Drama
episodes.description: Through Professor Frink's future machine, Bart and Lisa see their lives in the year 2013, where Bart steals Lisa's chances at going to an Ivy League school in order to impress a skater girl named Jenda while Homer (now separated from Marge and living in an underwater apartment) fights Krusty the Clown after Marge begins dating him.
episodes.notes: This episode's 2013 setting proved remarkably prescient, accurately predicting several real-world trends including the popularity of drone delivery and virtual reality technology that would indeed emerge by that year.

episodes.title: Don't Fear the Roofer
episodes.description: Homer meets a new friend, Ray the Roofer, but gets confined to a mental institution when his friends and family believe he made him up.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, but then the couch and family are revealed to be a painting that comes to life.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the few times The Simpsons directly parodied a recent Oscar-winning film, with Ray the Roofer serving as Homer's version of Russell Crowe's imaginary characters from A Beautiful Mind (2001).

episodes.title: The Heartbroke Kid
episodes.description: When Bart endangers his health by bingeing on vending-machine food, the Simpsons turn their home into a hostel in order to afford sending him to a forced starvation facility.
episodes.notes: This episode features Albert Brooks' second guest appearance as different characters, playing counselor Tab Watkins after previously voicing Jacques the bowling instructor in Season 1.

episodes.title: A Star Is Torn
episodes.description: Lisa agrees to enter a Krusty-sponsored "Li'l Starmaker" competition and panics when another contestant outperforms her.

episodes.title: Thank God It's Doomsday
episodes.description: Homer sees a movie about the end of the world and fears the same thing will happen in real life after seeing a chain of random occurrences and doing a complicated math equation that predicts the end will come on May 18th.
episodes.parody_of: Left Behind series and apocalyptic/rapture films
episodes.notes: This episode features one of the series' most elaborate mathematical sequences, with Homer's rapture calculations becoming a popular internet meme and spawning countless fan recreations of his apocalyptic formula.

episodes.title: Home Away from Homer
episodes.description: Flanders rents a room to two college girls who, without his knowledge, use the spare room to broadcast live softcore pornographic web videos of themselves.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the first times a major network primetime animated series directly addressed internet pornography and webcam culture, reflecting the mid-2000s boom in amateur online content creation.

episodes.title: The Father, the Son and the Holy Guest Star
episodes.description: Bart gets expelled from school and transfers to a Catholic school, where a hip priest named Father Sean tries to convert Bart and Homer to Catholicism, which worries Marge when she believes that Catholics do not go to the same heaven as Protestants and discovers that Catholic women do not use birth control.
episodes.notes: Liam Neeson guest starred as Father Sean, marking one of his rare comedic voice acting roles during the height of his dramatic film career.

episodes.title: The Bonfire of the Manatees
episodes.description: After Homer gets into trouble over his football gambling debts, he allows the Springfield Mafia to shoot a "snuggle film" in the house without Marge's permission. When she and the kids return home from a trip to "Santa's Village" and find production still going on, a furious Marge leaves for the beach and encounters a marine biologist named Caleb Thorn who has dedicated his life to saving manatees.
episodes.notes: This episode marked the first time The Simpsons directly addressed the adult film industry as a major plot point, pushing content boundaries further than previous episodes. The manatee conservation storyline reflected growing environmental awareness in mid-2000s television programming.

episodes.title: The Girl Who Slept Too Little
episodes.description: After the family successfully protest against a stamp museum being built next to their house, a cemetery is built near the Simpson house instead, making the normally unflappable Lisa realize that someone as smart as her can be afraid of something.

guests.character: Woman Introducing Milton Burkhart / Additional Voices (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marks a rare instance where Lisa, typically the show's voice of reason, confronts her own irrational fears in a horror-themed non-Halloween episode. The cemetery setting and supernatural elements made it one of the few regular-season episodes to blend The Simpsons' trademark humor with genuine horror tropes outside of the annual Treehouse of Horror specials.

episodes.title: Milhouse of Sand and Fog
episodes.description: Never one to miss the opportunity to make a quick buck, Homer cashes in on Maggie's bout of chicken pox. He decides to host a Pox Party and charges parents to infect their children.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XVI
episodes.description: Bart ends up in a coma after attempting to jump from the roof to the swimming pool at Spinster Arms Apartments, while attending a hunting party at Burns Manor, Homer panics and believes the prey is the hunting party, and after losing a Halloween costume contest to Dr Hibbert as Dracula, a real witch puts a spell on the people of Springfield.

guests.character: David / Female Robot / Witch / Agnes Skinner (voice)

episodes.parody_of: B.I.: Bartificial Intelligence parodies A.I.: Artificial Intelligence, Survival of the Fattest parodies The Most Dangerous Game, I've Grown a Costume on Your Face parodies various horror tropes

episodes.title: Marge's Son Poisoning
episodes.description: When Bart decides to spend a bit of time with Marge, it's not long before he's buying tea sets and singing duets. Has he turned into a mummy's boy?

guests.character: Tea House Waitress / Dolph / Agnes Skinner (voice)

episodes.title: See Homer Run
episodes.description: Homer becomes Springfield's safety guru and before long he's challenging Mayor Quimby for office. Until, that is, there's an embarrassing mishap with his costume.

guests.character: Dolph / Brandine / Miss Springfield / Crazy Cat Lady (voice)

episodes.title: The Last of the Red Hat Mamas
episodes.description: Marge is shunned after Homer has an altercation with the Easter Bunny at the mayor's egg hunt. A lonely Marge joins a women's group who plan to steal $1 million from Mr Burns.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Cheery Red Tomato (voice)
Mrs. Quimby / Miss Springfield / Additional Voices (voice)

episodes.title: The Italian Bob
episodes.description: Homer brings the whole family along when he is sent to Italy to pick up a new sports car for Mr. Burns, when they find themselves in a small town that Sideshow Bob has become the mayor of.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which turns into a giant mouth that swallows them.
episodes.notes: This episode marks Kelsey Grammer's first return as Sideshow Bob after a four-year absence, his longest gap between appearances in the series.

episodes.title: Simpsons Christmas Stories
episodes.description: The Simpsons re-enact the Nativity; a World War II tale stars Grampa, Mr. Burns and Santa; a musical features "The Nutcracker Suite."
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, then the camera pulls back to reveal they're part of a snow globe being shaken.
episodes.parody_of: The Nutcracker Suite and various Christmas stories

episodes.title: Homer's Paternity Coot
episodes.description: Mail from forty years earlier is discovered, and a letter from Homer Simpson's mother's old boyfriend states that he is Homer's true father. Homer sets out to find his supposed new father, leaving Abe behind.
episodes.notes: Guest star William H. Macy plays Mason Fairbanks, Homer's potential biological father, in a rare dramatic turn for the typically comedic series. The episode explores one of the show's most significant potential retcons to Homer's family history, though it ultimately preserves the status quo.

episodes.title: We're on the Road to D'ohwhere
episodes.description: After Bart steals a key from Principal Skinner's office and gets himself into hot water, Marge and Homer send him to a behavior modification camp.

episodes.title: My Fair Laddy
episodes.description: Mr. Krupt is the school’s new gym teacher and introduces Bart and the kids to “BOMBARDMENT!” Bart’s attempt to get back at the new gym teacher results in the destruction of Groundskeeper Willie’s shack and the family takes him in. Lisa decides to make Willie the focus of her science fair project and tries to turn him into a “proper gentleman” with surprising results. Meanwhile, Homer has ruined his last pair of blue pants and attempts to generate interest in them so the manufacturer will start making them again.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Janey Powell / Jimbo Jones (voice)

episodes.title: The Seemingly Never-Ending Story
episodes.description: The family is on a nature walk through “Carl’s Dad Caverns,” when Homer tries to get a piece from a stalactite. This results in the family falling through the cave floor and winds up with Homer getting stuck in the ceiling of one of the lower chambers of the cave. Marge, Bart and Maggie go off to find help, while Lisa stays behind and tells her father a story to pass the time. Her story involves running from a bighorn sheep and into Mr. Burn’s mansion. The pair evades the tenacious sheep by finding refuge in the mansion’s attic, where Mr. Burns tells her the story of how he came to work at Moe’s Tavern by losing a scavenger hunt with a rich Texan. While there he learned (and tells to Lisa) the tale of the Moe’s treasure. The stories within stories culminate in the fate of the treasure being determined in the cave.
episodes.notes: This episode's complex nested narrative structure of stories-within-stories directly parodies "The Arabian Nights," making it one of the most structurally ambitious episodes in the series' run.

episodes.title: Bart Has Two Mommies
episodes.description: The family is at a church fund-raiser, where Homer tries to ensure his rubber duck onto victory, but Flanders wins the big prize, which he doesn’t want and gives it to Marge. In return Marge agrees to watch Rod and Todd while Ned is attending a left-handed convention. While Marge is spending all her time with the Rod and Todd and trying to let them learn how to be boys; Homer takes Bart and Lisa to a home for former celebrity animals and a monkey kidnaps Bart. Marge’s ability to be a mother comes into question when Ned discovers what she is doing with his boys and the local news covers Bart’s current predicament.

episodes.title: Homer Simpson, This Is Your Wife
episodes.description: Homer is stoked for a party, where Lenny shows off his new plasma screen HD TV. Three days later and Homer still hasn’t left Lenny’s apartment. Lenny eventually gets him to leave and Marge enters them in a contest where they can win a plasma TV of their own. They don’t win, but 3rd place gets them a tour of the FOX studios, where Homer learns about a wife-swapping show where he can win enough money to buy his own plasma TV. Marge agrees to appear and they exchange wives with the Heathbar family. Charles Heathbar winds up falling in love with Marge (who is giving him what he needs), while his real wife Verity finds Homer detestable.
episodes.notes: This episode marked the first time The Simpsons directly parodied a specific reality TV show format, capitalizing on the mid-2000s wife-swapping show craze led by ABC's "Wife Swap.

episodes.title: Million Dollar Abie
episodes.description: Homer spearheads the effort to get pro-football to bring a new franchise to Springfield. The football commissioner, on his way to officially award the franchise, stops at the Simpson house where Grandpa (mistaking him for a burglar) attacks him. Springfield loses out and Abe becomes the town’s pariah. After trying and failing assisted suicide, Abe decides to live his life without fear and when the town decides to reuse their football stadium as a bullfighting ring, he volunteers to be the toreador. Ever the activist, granddaughter Lisa protests his new profession.

episodes.title: Kiss Kiss, Bang Bangalore
episodes.description: At the power plant a movie is shown where the workers endorse outsourcing and Mr. Burns announces that he is closing the plant and moving the work to India. Homer is the only union employee left on the payroll and he is sent to India to train the replacement workers, when he succeeds beyond expectations, he is given total control of the plant and declares himself a god. Meanwhile, Selma & Patty take Bart and Lisa to their MacGyver convention. When they discover Richard Dean Anderson doesn’t like MacGyver and much as they do, they kidnap him in retaliation. Anderson escapes only to enjoy the thrill and he turns himself back in so that he can escape again. When it happens over and over again, soon Patty and Selma are the ones who need a means of escape.
episodes.notes: This episode aired during The Simpsons' controversial period of tackling contemporary issues like outsourcing, marking one of the show's most direct commentaries on early 2000s economic anxieties. Richard Dean Anderson's guest appearance as himself cleverly subverted typical celebrity cameos by having the MacGyver star openly mock his own iconic role.

episodes.title: The Wettest Stories Ever Told
episodes.description: As the family waits for their food at “The Frying Dutchman,” they pass the time by telling stories. Lisa tells about the Mayflower’s journey to America, where Marge and her kids are Pilgrims and they are joined by a knave named Homer who joins their family to escape the authorities. Bart recounts the story of Mutiny on the Bounty featuring Skinner as Captain Bligh and Bart as Mr. Christian. Homer tells the tale of the maiden voyage of a cruise ship in the 1970s featuring of a ship that can’t flip over, which of course it does and the attempts of the survivors to get out of the ship alive.
episodes.parody_of: The Poseidon Adventure and maritime disaster films

episodes.title: Girls Just Want to Have Sums
episodes.description: The family attends "Stab-A-Lot: The Itchy and Scratchy Musical." The musical's director is a graduate of Springfield Elementary and Principal Skinner puts his foot in mouth when he makes sexist comments about her education and that of women in general. He tries to make the situation better, but only buries himself deeper and deeper until he is fired and replaced by Women's Educational Expert Melanie Upfoot. The first thing she does is split the school into girls and the boys. Lisa dislikes the way girls are being taught math and she ventures over to the boy's side of the school in search of a challenge. Disguising herself as Jake Boyman she starts attending the boy's school but while she finds the math problems more interesting, it is being a boy that is a greater challenge.
episodes.parody_of: Boys Don't Cry and gender identity themes
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of The Simpsons' earliest explorations of gender identity themes, with Lisa's extended disguise as "Jake Boyman" predating mainstream television's broader engagement with transgender storylines by several years.

episodes.title: Regarding Margie
episodes.description: Marge goes on a cleaning frenzy before the housekeeper she’s won the services of comes to the house. One spot remains and she mixes a number of toxic cleaners together to tackle the job. The noxious mixture knocks her out and she wakes up suffering from amnesia. Marge is taken home and the family tries to bring back her memory. She starts to get back memories of her children and other insignificant things, but when it comes to remembering her husband, nothing he does seems to jog her memory. When he can’t get her to remember him, he tries to win her all over again, especially after Patty and Selma get her back out on the dating scene.
episodes.parody_of: 50 First Dates and amnesia/memory loss romantic comedies

episodes.title: The Monkey Suit
episodes.description: Working through their checklists of things to do before summer ends, Lisa gets the family to go a museum. At the Springfield Museum of Natural History there is an exhibit Lisa is excited to see on Women’s History turns into an exhibit on the history of weapons, which excites Homer and Bart. When Flanders sees the exhibit of evolution and another that mocks creationism, he raises an alarm. Reverend Lovejoy gets Skinner to have creationism taught in school. Lisa is the only one who holds onto the scientific theory. She brings the topic up at the town meeting and the town votes to teach only one theory, creationism. Lisa decides to hold her own class on evolution, until she is arrested. The ACLU gives her a lawyer who is up against a slick Southern lawyer, but ultimately it’s a family member that is a key to her defense.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Rod Flanders / Janey Powell (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode parodies the famous 1925 Scopes "Monkey" Trial and the play/film "Inherit the Wind," with Lisa defending evolution against creationism in a courtroom setting that mirrors one of America's most significant legal battles over science education.

episodes.title: Marge and Homer Turn a Couple Play
episodes.description: When Homer hears that the Isotopes are on a winning streak, he takes the family to the game. The first baseman’s game is going bad, caused by his sexy singing wife Tabitha embarrassing him during the game’s national anthem. Buck Mitchell sees Marge and Homer’s kiss on the game’s Kiss-Cam and comes to them for marital advice. They start working with the couple and Buck’s baseball career turns around, but it and the Simpson marriage goes awry again when Buck catches Homer giving Tabitha a neck massage. While Marge and Homer try keeping their marriage together they also try to get Buck and Tabitha back together.

guests.character: Howard K. Duff VIII / Don Bookner (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode features one of the series' rare explorations of professional sports psychology through marriage counseling, marking a unique crossover between Homer's sports obsession and relationship dynamics that would influence later episodes dealing with celebrity couples.

episodes.title: The Mook, the Chef, the Wife and Her Homer
episodes.description: Lisa's friendship with Fat Tony's son Michael prompts mob boss Tony to invite the Simpsons over for dinner. Michael says he'd rather be a chef than take over the family business, but that's before Tony is shot by a rival mobster.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the death of Fat Tony, ending Joe Mantegna's 15-year run as the character, though he would return the following season voicing Fat Tony's cousin Fit Tony who assumes the same name and mannerisms.

episodes.title: Jazzy and the Pussycats
episodes.description: A child psychiatrist suggests drumming to channel Bart's energy, and the boy proves he's got the beat when he shows some talent on the skins.

episodes.title: Please Homer, Don't Hammer 'Em
episodes.description: Marge reads Homer's carpentry books and becomes very handy at fixing things. Gender bias keeps the townspeople from accepting a female carpenter, so Marge pretends that Homer does the work. Meanwhile, Bart torments Skinner when he learns the principal has an extreme allergy to peanuts.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XVII
episodes.description: The 17th annual Halloween trilogy. Included: "Married to the Blob," in which a meteor turns Homer into a monster; "You Gotta Know When to Golem," about a monster from Jewish folklore; and "The Day the Earth Looked Stupid," about an alien invasion.
episodes.parody_of: The Blob, Jewish folklore/The Golem of Prague, War of the Worlds

episodes.title: G.I. (Annoyed Grunt)
episodes.description: Homer is bamboozled by Army recruiters and winds up in basic training, where he runs afoul of a tough-minded colonel and is assigned to play the enemy in war games.
episodes.parody_of: Military films and war movies like Full Metal Jacket
episodes.notes: This episode features one of the show's most extensive military parodies, directly spoofing Full Metal Jacket's drill sergeant scenes with Homer enduring basic training humiliation.

episodes.title: Moe'N'a Lisa
episodes.description: Lisa sees poetry in the notes Moe sticks to the walls of his hotel room, so she sends his work to a poetry journal, which publishes it, making Moe the toast of the literary circuit.

guests.character: Eloise / Maya Angelou / Woman at Festival (voice)

episodes.title: Ice Cream of Margie (With the Light Blue Hair)
episodes.description: Homer is fired and decides to buy an ice-cream truck. Marge makes use of the mountains of Popsicle sticks from Homer's ice-cream consumption by creating sculptures of Springfield citizens with them, leading to a feature about her on the news.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Prof. Stein (voice)

episodes.title: The Haw-Hawed Couple
episodes.description: When Bart ends up the only guest at Nelson's birthday party, the bully decides he's his best friend and goes about protecting Bart in a parody of "Goodfellas." Meanwhile, Homer reads Lisa to sleep and debates on whether to reveal the book's sad ending to her.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Richard (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marked a rare exploration of Nelson's vulnerable side, transforming the one-dimensional bully into a complex character desperate for friendship. The Goodfellas parody featured some of the most sophisticated cinematic homages in the series, complete with voice-over narration and carefully recreated iconic scenes.

episodes.title: Kill Gil, Volumes I & II
episodes.description: Homer jumps into a brawl during a holiday ice-skating show. To escape the melee, Marge takes the family Christmas shopping, where a chance encounter with Gil leads to bad luck for Gil and the Simpson clan.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Real Estate Agent #3 (voice)
Candy Cane / Melina Costington / Additional Voices (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marked one of Gil Gunderson's rare starring roles, centering an entire plot around the perpetually unlucky salesman who typically appeared only in brief cameos since his debut in Season 9.

episodes.title: The Wife Aquatic
episodes.description: Selma and Patti's home movies make Marge nostalgic for the Barnacle Bay vacations of her youth, so Homer takes the family there, but the area has changed since the Bouviers were little girls.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Rod Flanders (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode features one of the most elaborate musical sequences in the series, with Homer's sea shanty "Married to the Sea" becoming a standout moment that showcases the show's ambitious musical storytelling in its later seasons.

episodes.title: Revenge Is a Dish Best Served Three Times
episodes.description: Homer learns three lessons about revenge from stories told by Marge, Lisa and Bart that parody "The Count of Monte Cristo," "Revenge of the Nerds" and "Batman Begins."

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Ham (voice)

episodes.parody_of: The Count of Monte Cristo, Revenge of the Nerds, and Batman Begins
episodes.notes: This anthology-style episode was one of the first non-Treehouse of Horror episodes to feature three completely separate parody segments, establishing a format that would be used sparingly in later seasons.

episodes.title: Little Big Girl
episodes.description: Lisa pretends to be Native American for multicultural day at school. Meanwhile, Bart finds love with an older woman.

guests.character: Woman in Movie / Woman at Courthouse / Darcy's Mother (voice)
Dolph / Cletus's Son / Brandine / Japanese Woman (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the first times The Simpsons directly addressed cultural appropriation as a central theme, with Lisa's Native American impersonation reflecting growing real-world conversations about identity and representation in the mid-2000s.

episodes.title: Springfield Up
episodes.description: A documentary filmmaker who interviewed a group of Springfield schoolchildren 32 years ago has followed up at eight-year intervals to film how their lives have unfolded.
episodes.notes: This episode parodies Michael Apted's acclaimed "Up" documentary series, which follows the same group of British children every seven years from age 7 to adulthood. The Simpsons version condenses the concept by checking in on Springfield kids at eight-year intervals over 32 years.

episodes.title: Yokel Chords
episodes.description: Bart has to see a psychiatrist after he starts a panic at school by telling a story about a murderous cafeteria worker. Meanwhile, Lisa tries to civilize Cletus Spuckler's hillbilly children, with mixed results.

episodes.title: Rome-Old and Juli-Eh
episodes.description: Selma and Grampa embark on a May-December romance, and Bart and Lisa get into trouble with deliverymen over cardboard boxes.

guests.character: Employment Agent / Admitted Fraud #2 / Angry Woman (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the rare romantic storylines for Grampa Simpson, pairing him with Selma in an unusual May-December romance that parodies Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet with geriatric characters.

episodes.title: Homerazzi
episodes.description: After a celebrity scandal is accidentally captured in a family photo, Homer decides to become a paparazzo.

guests.character: Little Boy / Paris Texan / Mayor Quimby's Mother (voice)

episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and it turns into a monster that eats them.
episodes.parody_of: paparazzi culture and celebrity tabloid journalism

episodes.title: Marge Gamer
episodes.description: Marge goes online for the first time and becomes obsessed with a role-playing game that Bart and many of their neighbors are playing. Meanwhile, Lisa develops her own obsession--for soccer--and Homer becomes a referee.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Janey (voice)

episodes.parody_of: World of Warcraft and MMORPG games
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the first mainstream animated TV parodies of massively multiplayer online role-playing games, predicting the cultural impact of World of Warcraft which had launched just three years earlier.

episodes.title: The Boys of Bummer
episodes.description: Bart goes from hero to goat on his Little League team after an error in the championship game. Meanwhile, Homer falls asleep on a bed in a department store. When he's awakened, he raves about the mattress and is given a job as a salesman.

guests.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Jimbo Jones / Audience (voice)
Agnes Skinner / Batter / Dolph / Additional Voices (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the few times Bart faces sustained community-wide hatred and social ostracism, exploring darker psychological territory typically reserved for more serious characters like Frank Grimes.

episodes.title: Crook and Ladder
episodes.description: Homer, Apu, Moe and Skinner volunteer as firemen after Homer's sleepwalking accident injures the town's firefighters. They do some light looting at the fires to make up for not being compensated, but Marge and Lisa shame them into mending their ways.

episodes.title: Stop or My Dog Will Shoot!
episodes.description: The Simpsons' pooch, Santa's Little Helper, becomes a police dog.

guests.character: Voice on Radio / Brandine / Dolph / Shoquelle (voice)

episodes.parody_of: Police procedural shows and movies about police dogs

episodes.title: 24 Minutes
episodes.description: In a parody of "24," the school's Counter Truancy Unit tries to stop three sixth-graders planning to disrupt the school bake sale with a stink bomb.
episodes.notes: This marked the first time The Simpsons parodied a contemporary primetime drama in real-time, with "24" still airing new episodes when this parody premiered.

episodes.title: You Kent Always Say What You Want
episodes.description: Newsman Kent Brockman is fired for swearing on the air when Homer dumps coffee in his lap, but Lisa gets Brockman back in the game with her Webcam.
episodes.notes: This episode aired during the early days of YouTube and web video, making it one of the first mainstream TV shows to feature webcasting as a central plot device. Kent Brockman's uncensored web show reflected the era's shift toward unfiltered online content that bypassed traditional broadcast standards.

episodes.title: He Loves to Fly and He D'ohs
