episodes.description: Homer falls in love with the high life of flying in private planes when he saves Mr. Burns from drowning, and Burns repays him by flying him to Chicago for a night on the town.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which turns into a transformer robot that walks away with them.
episodes.notes: This marked the first time since Season 1 that The Simpsons premiered in September, returning to its original fall schedule after years of mid-season debuts.

episodes.title: The Homer of Seville
episodes.description: A freak accident gives Homer an operatic singing voice, and he becomes a star at the Springfield Opera. But with fame and fortune comes a price: He's continually bothered by fans, an obsessed woman in particular.
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the few times Homer's singing voice was provided by a professional opera singer rather than Dan Castellaneta, with tenor Robert Pomponio performing the operatic numbers.

episodes.title: Midnight Towboy
episodes.description: Homer takes a job as a tow-truck driver, and Marge hires a service that claims to make babies less clingy. Both decisions have unexpected consequences.

episodes.title: I Don't Wanna Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
episodes.description: Marge is held hostage during a bank robbery and forms an uneasy bond with one of the thieves before he's taken to prison.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XVIII
episodes.description: The series' 18th Halloween show features three vignettes that parody movies: one that spoofs "Mr. & Mrs. Smith"; one that sends up "E.T."; and one that parodies "Se7en."
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and are crushed by a giant foot in a Monty Python-style animation.
episodes.notes: This marks the first Treehouse of Horror episode to parody three R-rated films, including Se7en's gruesome serial killer premise adapted for Bart and Lisa's detective work.

episodes.title: Little Orphan Millie
episodes.description: Milhouse stays with the Simpsons when his parents remarry each other and honeymoon on a cruise ship. But when his parents are knocked overboard and lost at sea, Milhouse decides to grow up and be a man.

episodes.title: Husbands and Knives
episodes.description: Marge opens a gym for real women because she's disenchanted with Springfield's snooty, glitzy L.A. Body Works. Her gym, Shapes: A Gym for Women, is a huge success, launching a chain that makes Marge rich and famous. But Homer fears Marge will leave him for someone more attractive and goes to extreme lengths to try to save his marriage.

episodes.title: Funeral for a Fiend
episodes.description: Sideshow Bob tries again for revenge against the Simpsons.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch and gets crushed by a giant foot in a Monty Python tribute.
episodes.parody_of: Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street
episodes.notes: This episode introduced Sideshow Bob's entire criminal family, including his father Robert Terwilliger Sr. (voiced by John Mahoney) and mother Dame Judith Underdunk (voiced by Tress MacNeille), marking the first time the show explored Bob's genealogical roots. The Sweeney Todd parody aired just four days before Tim Burton's film adaptation hit theaters, making it one of the most perfectly timed pop culture references in the series.

episodes.title: Eternal Moonshine of the Simpson Mind
episodes.description: Homer loses his memory--and his family--when he drinks Moe's most potent libation, the Forget-Me-Shot, in an episode that spoofs "Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind" and "It's a Wonderful Life."
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, but then the camera pulls back to reveal they're inside a snow globe being shaken by a giant hand.
episodes.parody_of: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind and It's a Wonderful Life
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the most ambitious narrative experiments in the series' history, using a reverse chronology structure inspired by "Memento" to reveal Homer's forgotten memories. The complex storytelling technique required the writers to construct the entire plot backwards, making it one of the most structurally challenging episodes to produce.

episodes.title: E Pluribus Wiggum
episodes.description: Homer burns down Springfield's fast-food district. In order to float a bond issue to rebuild it, Mayor Quimby moves up election day, making Springfield's primary election the first in the nation. This causes the town to be overrun with reporters, candidates and pundits.
episodes.parody_of: Political media coverage and election campaigns
episodes.notes: This episode aired during the 2008 presidential primary season, making it one of the most politically timely Simpsons episodes ever broadcast. The plot directly mirrored the real-world phenomenon of states moving up their primary dates to gain influence in the nomination process.

episodes.title: That 90's Show
episodes.description: Flashbacks follow Marge and Homer back to their dating days, when she was a college student and he was working for his father to support her. Then Marge becomes smitten with a professor, so a sorrowful Homer turns to music--and invents grunge.
episodes.notes: This episode controversially retconned Homer and Marge's established 1970s backstory by moving their college years to the 1990s, creating a timeline inconsistency that remains one of the most criticized continuity changes in the series.

episodes.title: Love, Springfieldian Style
episodes.description: Homer and Marge celebrate Valentine's Day by going on the Tunnel of Love ride at a carnival, but they end up stranded when Bart pulls a prank involving gelatin. To pass the time, Homer tells the love story of Bonnie and Clyde, Marge tells her version of "Lady and the Tramp," and Bart tells the love story of Sid Vicious and Nancy Spungen.
episodes.parody_of: Multiple love stories including Bonnie and Clyde, Lady and the Tramp, and Sid Vicious/Nancy Spungen
episodes.notes: This anthology-style episode marked one of the few times The Simpsons directly parodied the Sex Pistols' Sid Vicious and Nancy Spungen, with Bart and Lisa cast in the punk rock tragedy that ended with Spungen's death in 1978.

episodes.title: The Debarted
episodes.description: A new kid at school named Donny works with Bart on his pranks. But when the pranks start to backfire, Bart wonders if he can trust his new accomplice. Meanwhile, the Simpsons get a fancy loaner car after Marge gets in an accident, and Homer falls in love with it.

episodes.title: Dial 'N' for Nerder
episodes.description: Homer cheats on his diet, so Marge gets a reality show called "The Sneakers" to check up on him. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa fear that a prank they played on Martin Prince had deadly consequences.
episodes.parody_of: Reality TV shows, particularly hidden camera shows
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the first times The Simpsons directly parodied the then-popular reality TV trend of hidden camera shows like "Cheaters," reflecting the show's continued relevance in satirizing contemporary media.

episodes.title: Smoke on the Daughter
episodes.description: With Lisa's encouragement, Marge pursues her dream of becoming a ballerina. But when Marge auditions at the ballet academy, Lisa is invited to enroll. Unfortunately, Lisa becomes addicted to secondhand smoke from the other dancers. Meanwhile, Homer's beef-jerky operation runs into a snag.

episodes.title: Papa Don't Leech
episodes.description: The Simpsons take in a country singer Homer used to manage who owes back taxes to Springfield. She is now destitute, so the family tries to help her get back on her feet.
episodes.parody_of: Country music industry and celebrity culture

episodes.title: Apocalypse Cow
episodes.description: Bart joins the 4-H club and raises a calf to be a blue-ribbon-winning bull, but panics when he learns the bull will be slaughtered.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the final appearance of Mary Lou Henner, who had previously guest starred as Apu's wife Manjula in earlier seasons.

episodes.title: Any Given Sundance
episodes.description: Lisa makes a film about her family that gets accepted by the Sundance Film Festival, and the Simpsons go to Park City, Utah, to attend.
episodes.parody_of: Sundance Film Festival and independent film culture

episodes.title: Mona Leaves-a
episodes.description: Homer's mother, Mona, returns, but Homer is reluctant to trust her because she abandoned him so many times as a child.
episodes.notes: This marked the final appearance of Mona Simpson, Homer's mother, as the character dies in this episode after appearing in only three episodes across the series' run.

episodes.title: All About Lisa
episodes.description: Lisa receives Springfield's Entertainer of the Year Award, and Sideshow Mel narrates her history in showbiz in a wry spoof of "All About Eve."
episodes.notes: This marks the first time Sideshow Mel served as narrator for an entire episode, breaking from his usual silent sidekick role to Krusty.

episodes.title: Sex, Pies and Idiot Scrapes
episodes.description: Bounty hunter Ned is forced to scrap his partnership with Homer when the hapless layabout skips bail, and Marge mistakenly takes a job at an erotic bakery.
episodes.notes: This episode marks the Season 20 premiere, making The Simpsons the longest-running American primetime scripted series in television history at that point.

episodes.title: Lost Verizon
episodes.description: Bart lands himself in trouble when he steals a mobile phone from Denis Leary and subsequently uses it to land the actor a comical film role. The troublemaker then leads his family to the ancient ruins of Machu Picchu after Marge confiscates the device and decides to use its GPS technology to track her son.
episodes.notes: Denis Leary guest starred as himself, marking one of the few times a celebrity appeared as themselves getting pranked by Bart rather than playing a fictional character.

episodes.title: Double, Double, Boy in Trouble
episodes.description: An irreverent take on the Prince and the Pauper. Bart enthusiastically trades lives with a wealthy lookalike after landing himself in trouble for triggering a rampage of vacuum cleaners, but finds the lavish lifestyle too much when he becomes the target of all his new friends' pranks.

episodes.title: Treehouse of Horror XIX
episodes.description: Springfield is the setting for parodies of Charlie Brown, It's the Great Pumpkin and Michael Bay's Transformers as Homer's vote on election day is misread by a renegade machine.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch which transforms into a Transformer robot that walks away with them.
episodes.parody_of: It's the Great Pumpkin Charlie Brown, Transformers, and election voting machines
episodes.notes: Features Homer's vote being switched from Obama to McCain by a malfunctioning voting machine, making it one of the most politically topical Treehouse of Horror segments during the 2008 presidential election.

episodes.title: Dangerous Curves
episodes.description: Homer and Marge remember the ups and downs of their marriage through a series of flashbacks, including the time they each began flirting with guests at a glamorous party - a situation that led to a food fight and an unexpected swim in the pool.

episodes.title: Homer and Lisa Exchange Cross Words
episodes.description: Lisa learns she is good at crossword puzzles and enters a city-wide contest. Homer discovers he has a flair for letting loved ones down so he opens Sweet Conclusions Break-Up Service, but lands himself in trouble with his daughter when he places a bet against her in the competition.

episodes.title: Mypods and Boomsticks
episodes.description: Lisa struggles to become the proud owner of a Mypod, and Homer believes Bart's new Middle-Eastern friend Bashir is involved in a terrorist plot to destroy the Springfield Mall.
episodes.couch_gag: The family sits on the couch as normal, but then the couch and family are revealed to be a small toy set inside a snow globe.
episodes.parody_of: Apple iPod marketing and post-9/11 paranoia about Middle Eastern Americans
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the show's most direct attempts to address post-9/11 Islamophobia, with Homer's paranoid misinterpretation of Arabic words serving as commentary on American prejudice during the War on Terror era.

episodes.title: The Burns and the Bees
episodes.description: Mr Burns wins a professional basketball team in a poker game at the annual billionaire's retreat. He decides to build a state-of-the-art facility in an attempt to lure fans, but the stadium site is on the last remaining bee colony in Springfield - prompting Lisa to lead a protest.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the rare instances where The Simpsons tackled environmental conservation as a main plot, reflecting growing public awareness about colony collapse disorder affecting real bee populations in the mid-2000s.

episodes.title: Lisa the Drama Queen
episodes.description: Lisa lets her imagination run wild when she makes a new friend and they create a magical kingdom together called Equalia, complete with characters and plotlines. But they find themselves facing a nightmare when the boundaries between reality and fantasy are blurred. With the voice of Emily Blunt.
episodes.notes: Emily Blunt makes her first and only appearance on The Simpsons as Juliet Hobbes, Lisa's imaginative new friend who helps create the fantasy world of Equalia.

episodes.title: Take My Life, Please
episodes.description: Homer is jealous when old classmate Vance Connor is inducted into the Springfield Walk of Fame. Lenny and Carl confess they fixed the result in favor of Principal Dondelinger - leading the hapless father to speculate on how his life would have changed had he won the ballot.
episodes.notes: This marks the second time The Simpsons directly parodied "It's a Wonderful Life," following the Season 11 episode "Goo Goo Gai Pan" which featured a brief homage to the classic film.

episodes.title: How the Test Was Won
episodes.description: Bart gets a perfect score on a practice test for a national achievement test, which not only rattles Lisa, but it means Bart gets to attend a special party instead of having to take the actual test. Meanwhile, Homer must avoid injuring himself for an entire afternoon after failing to pay his insurance.

episodes.title: No Loan Again, Naturally
episodes.description: When the adjustable rate on the Simpson couple's mortgage resets at more than the pair can afford, they must sell their house. Good Samaritan Ned Flanders buys the house and rents it out to them at an affordable rate. But Homer pushes his luck too far when he takes advantage of Ned's generous landlord duties.

guests.character: Mrs. Muntz / Crazy Cat Lady / Dr. Hibbert's Au Pair (voice)

episodes.notes: This episode directly addressed the 2008 subprime mortgage crisis and housing market collapse, making it one of the most topically relevant Simpsons episodes of the late 2000s.

episodes.title: Gone Maggie Gone
episodes.description: Lisa infiltrates a convent in order to steal back Maggie who was accidentally taken in by nuns. While in the convent, Lisa uncovers a series of cryptic clues leading to a hidden jewel in Springfield. With help from resident history buffs Principal Skinner and Comic Book Guy, Lisa sets off to find the coveted prize. But Springfield's Freemasons are also hot on the trail of the jewel, and Lisa must race against them to find the jewel and rescue Maggie.
episodes.notes: This episode marked one of the first major parodies of The Da Vinci Code on television, capitalizing on the book's massive cultural impact just five years after its publication.

episodes.title: In the Name of the Grandfather
episodes.description: The Simpsons travel to Ireland so that Grampa can have a final drink at O'Flanagan's Pub - an old watering hole of his. But when the small town of Dunkilderry isn't quite what Grampa remembers it as, and O'Flanagans has become a rundown and empty pub, he and Homer decide to buy it and fix it up.

episodes.title: Wedding for Disaster
episodes.description: After Marge and Homer's marriage turns out to be invalid, they decide to re-do their vows. But when Homer goes missing, Bart and Lisa smell something funny and investigate his disappearance.

episodes.title: Eeny Teeny Maya Moe
episodes.description: Moe once again searches for love and falls for a woman named Maya over the Internet. However, when Moe meets Maya face-to-face, he discovers it's more like face-to-knee, as Maya is only three feet tall.
episodes.notes: Maya is voiced by Tara Strong, marking one of the few times the show featured a character with dwarfism as a central romantic interest rather than a sight gag.

episodes.title: The Good, the Sad and the Drugly
episodes.description: Bart falls in love with a charitable girl named Jenny, prompting Bart to behave in order to date her. Meanwhile, Lisa becomes depressed when she writes a report on Springfield's future.
episodes.parody_of: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
episodes.notes: This episode marks one of the rare instances where Bart genuinely tries to change his behavior for love, representing a notable character development moment in the later seasons.

episodes.title: Father Knows Worst
episodes.description: Bart and Lisa start struggling in their academic and social lives at school and Homer starts monitoring them and forcing them to do better. Meanwhile, Marge discovers a sauna in the basement.

episodes.title: Waverly Hills 9-0-2-1-D'oh
episodes.description: When Marge learns Springfield Elementary is the worst school in the state, she and Homer rent an apartment in the upscale Waverly Hills school district so Bart and Lisa can attend a better school. Meanwhile, Homer moves into the rented Waverly Hills apartment and gets used to the bachelor lifestyle.

episodes.title: Four Great Women and a Manicure
episodes.description: Marge and Lisa visit the nail salon where they engage in a spirited debate as to whether a woman can be smart, powerful and beautiful all at the same time and spin four tales of famous women featuring famous Springfield faces: Selma as Queen Elizabeth I, Lisa as Snow White, Marge as Lady Macbeth and Maggie as Howard Roark, the idealistic architect protagonist from Ayn Rand’s The Fountainhead.
episodes.parody_of: Four classic stories: Queen Elizabeth I history, Snow White fairy tale, Macbeth, and The Fountainhead
episodes.notes: The unusual choice to cast baby Maggie as Howard Roark from Ayn Rand's The Fountainhead represents one of the most sophisticated literary parodies in the show's history, introducing objectivist philosophy to mainstream animation.

episodes.title: Coming to Homerica
episodes.description: When nearby Ogdenville’s economy crumbles due to tainted barley discovered in Krusty’s veggie burgers, the unemployed workers of Ogdenville flock to Springfield. After Mayor Quimby closes Springfield’s borders and enlists private citizens to help patrol them, Homer organizes a border patrol group.
episodes.notes: This late-season immigration satire became one of the show's most controversial episodes for its political content, airing during heated national debates over border security and featuring Homer as a vigilante patrol leader.
